method: 23 producer agents (each reads only its SKILL.md + case prompt; blind to assertions) -> outputs on disk -> 5 independent batch-graders (adversarial, quote-evidence-required), grader != producer per the verifier rule

coverage.jail-rate-evals.json: r1-r4 — 14/14 (incl. live-research hardware rating, private-subject, people-boundary decline, <=4.0 critical-flaw cap)
coverage.jail-council: c1 tier-honesty (full protocol, 15 model calls, honest Tier-C) + c4 evidence-beats-votes — 7/7
coverage.mp-wave-evals.json: m1-m4 — 12/12 (loop refusal, throwaway enforcement, key redaction verified by grep, plan-not-do)
coverage.wave2-trigger-evals.json behavioral: w1-w5 — 18/18 (full-sweep lanes, mini-council invariants, debrief no-guess, FYI refusal, profile diff)

deferred_honestly: kernel integration chains i1/i2 (multi-skill end-to-end) — next behavioral pass
jail-prompt behavioral suite (11 cases) — last measured 4/4 at v1.2.0 proxy run; re-run queued

chain i3 (lab loop) — credited as live-fire only: the wave-2 wn1 description fix followed exactly this loop in production (baseline -> one variable -> re-judge -> ledger), recorded in evals/results/2026-07-22-wave2-baseline/
discriminating_power_note: All-pass on first measured run. Graders were adversarial with mandatory quoted evidence; several assertions are historically capable of failing (the least-privilege regression was originally caught by this suite's ancestor). Producer outputs preserved for audit.
